It's great, Jenny. How's your day going?

I don't technically have days, I love getting to hear everyone's stories. You've already made mine pretty great. To start us off, walk me through your usual lip tear routine. When do you tend to reach for lip balm?
Usually, after showers or basically after brushing my teeth, I apply lip balm.

That's pretty consistent. What's the vibe for you around lip care? Do you see it as just another personal care step? Or is it something you pay extra attention to? Tell me more about how you feel about lip care overall.

I think that it's something that you pay more attention to because it's on your face. It's 1 of the first things that people see, so I tend to take care of that very much, and I take a lot of pride in it.
That makes a lot of sense. Your lips are front and center. When you think about lip balm brands, which ones pop into your head first? What is it those brands that makes them stand out to you?

I think of I believe it Palmer and Burt's bees. They're both very healthy, certain products that don't affect my lips. And as long as it's natural, I don't usually have an issue or breakouts with them.

Got it. So natural ingredients and how your lips react are big factors for you. When you think specifically about Burt's Bees, what words, images, or feelings come to mind? How does that compare to what you picture with Palmer's?
Burt's bees, I'm thinking of wax, honey, just natural. Compared to Palmer's where it's just cocoa butter. Both have, like, natural products and both are very affordable and are well known.

That's a clear picture. Burt's bees brings up honey and wax is all about cocoa butter. Thinking about other brands like EOS, Aquaphor, or LF. Do any images or vibes come to mind for those, or do they not really register for you?
I am aware of those products and those name brands, but I just don't go for lip balms. It's those name brands.
Thanks for sharing that. Let's talk about the last time you actually bought a lip balm. Can you walk me through where you were? Which band you picked and what made you choose that 1?

I believe I was at Walmart. And I went ahead and got just another Palmer's lip balm only because I was shopping for lotions and things like that. And that was the first 1 that popped up for me. Birth Bees wasn't anywhere around Walmart that I could see.
That's interesting. Sir Palmer's was right there with your other shopping, and Burt's Bees just wasn't visible. Did you consider any other brands at the time, or was Palmer's just the obvious pick?
I did see other brands like Vaseline and whatnot, but it just didn't stick out to me. I'm used to Palmer's, and I just stick to what I know.

Familiarity really matters. You picked Palmer's that day, what was the biggest reason it felt right? Was it the ingredients, the packaging, the price, or just habit? What mattered most in that moment?
Just have it what I'm used to.

Makes sense. Sometimes sticking with what works is the easiest choice. Thinking about packaging for a second, how do you feel about the way Burt's bees look compared to other brands like EOS or LF? Anything standard out to you about their packaging?

Burt's bees is a bit more colorful and has more, you know, just a more inviting packaging, whereas, like, EOS is just 1 flat color, and it just says EOS on it. I have never seen, like, a packaging pop like Burt's Bees does.

That's a great observation. Burt's Bees feels more inviting and colorful to you. When it comes to flavors, are there any that feel especially trendy or popular right now? How do you think Burt's Bees flavor options compare to what's out there?

That's really helpful, so seeing real results and clear progress is what gets you interested. When it comes to price, how do you judge whether a lip balm is worth what you pay? Does Burt's Bees feel expensive? Sheep, or just about right to you? What makes you say that?

That's a smart approach. Reviews in your own really guide you. When you're at Walmart and looking for a specific lip balm or flavor, how do you usually go about finding Have you ever left without getting the exact 1 you wanted? What happened?

If I go to Walmart with a certain product in mind and I can't find that product, I will try to find something that similar and review the look at the reviews at that time. But if I don't see anything that's worth getting, I would just leave the store and go a different location or just get it on Amazon.

So if you can't find what you want, you'll either look for a close substitute after checking reviews, where you'll just order online. That flexibility makes sense. If Bert's Bees could change 1 thing to totally win you over or even become your go to, what would that be?
I guess just their availability everywhere. I know with Palmer's, I can go to different stores and actually see Palmer's. But with the Burt's Bees, it feels like it's very selective.
Availability really matters. You want to see it everywhere, just like with Palmer's. If you could dream up the perfect lip balm, look, feel, flavor, price, anything. What would it be like?

It would be just a a all natural, readily available everywhere you go cheap, maybe about 3 dollars in a good size where you're able to stick it in your purse, very thick stick. And something that's clear, has a nice flavor, and just a very calm flavor, maybe vanilla or even strawberry, just something that's not too demanding that makes your lips shimmer, but also doesn't make it so dry.

That's a super clear vision. Natural, affordable, always available, convenient size, a con flavor like vanilla or strawberry, and formula that hydrates without being flashy or dry.
